page: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n1 server family. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 120 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as shown by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores landing in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests also show average performance, with read-only large block speeds at 101.81 GB/sec and write speeds at 46.41 GB/sec.

Given its balanced resource allocation and average multi-threaded performance, this server is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded software compilation. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference, where it exhibits poor performance. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, it offers a dense allocation of dedicated vCPUs and memory for legacy or standard enterprise workloads that do not require high single-core clock speeds or local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-32 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) operating at 2.3 GHz, paired with 120 GB of RAM. It features no local storage and no bundled GPUs. Benchmarks reveal a clear tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%, while multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently reach average tiers. For instance, its Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores sit in the middle 50%. This server is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and standard database operations that benefit from parallel processing rather than high single-thread speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-32 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 vCPUs, 120 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core speeds.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-standard-32 is a general-purpose server with 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and average multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A multi-core general-purpose virtual machine featuring balanced memory allocation and steady multi-threaded performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
120 GB of RAM providing 3.75 GB per core
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Weak single-core and single-threaded processing capabilities
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators
Best suited for static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded compilation
